  cs.academic_year AS academic_year,
  AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric(5,2) AS avg_professor_rating,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS eval_count
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
  AND cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY cs.academic_year
ORDER BY
  (CASE WHEN cs.academic_year ~ '^[0-9]{4}' THEN substring(cs.academic_year from '^[0-9]{4}')::int ELSE NULL END) ASC NULLS LAST,
  cs.academic_year ASC;","validationIssues":["academic_year is encoded as ordinal/text — ensure the axis follows the SQL chronological order (e.g., set explicit x.sort based on the SQL ordering or convert/normalize academic_year to a temporal/parsed field) because lexicographic ordering could misorder seasons like '2019/2020' vs '2020/2021'.","If the user wanted per-professor trends rather than an overall average, the current chart is aggregated to a single line; include a professor identifier as a color/grouping dimension to show separate lines."]},"historySummary":"Attempt 1: passed","latest":{"sqlDraft":{"reasoning":"Aggregate average professor_rating by academic_year using confirmed join path (course_evaluations -> enrollments -> course_sections). visualization.title: Average Professor Rating by Academic Year

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  cs.academic_year AS academic_year,
  AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric(5,2) AS avg_professor_rating,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS eval_count
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
  AND cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY cs.academic_year
ORDER BY
  (CASE WHEN cs.academic_year ~ '^[0-9]{4}' THEN substring(cs.academic_year from '^[0-9]{4}')::int ELSE NULL END) ASC NULLS LAST,
  cs.academic_year ASC;

issues: academic_year is encoded as ordinal/text — ensure the axis follows the SQL chronological order (e.g., set explicit x.sort based on the SQL ordering or convert/normalize academic_year to a temporal/parsed field) because lexicographic ordering could misorder seasons like '2019/2020' vs '2020/2021'.

If the user wanted per-professor trends rather than an overall average, the current chart is aggregated to a single line; include a professor identifier as a color/grouping dimension to show separate lines.

visualization.title: Average Professor Rating by Academic Year

result.sql:
SELECT
  cs.academic_year AS academic_year,
  AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric(5,2) AS avg_professor_rating,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS eval_count
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
WHERE ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
  AND cs.academic_year IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY cs.academic_year
ORDER BY
  (CASE WHEN cs.academic_year ~ '^[0-9]{4}' THEN substring(cs.academic_year from '^[0-9]{4}')::int ELSE NULL END) ASC NULLS LAST,
  cs.academic_year ASC;

issues: academic_year is encoded as ordinal/text — ensure the axis follows the SQL chronological order (e.g., set explicit x.sort based on the SQL ordering or convert/normalize academic_year to a temporal/parsed field) because lexicographic ordering could misorder seasons like '2019/2020' vs '2020/2021'.

If the user wanted per-professor trends rather than an overall average, the current chart is aggregated to a single line; include a professor identifier as a color/grouping dimension to show separate lines.
